failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM prompt with explicit stagnation detection and role-switching directives
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 90 - Regression. Enhanced LLM prompt to detect stagnation and force role changes more aggressively (alpha.0 cyborg architecture parity attempt). Caused performance drop, particularly on seeds 44 (-23%) and 46 (-16%). The more directive prompt may have caused excessive role switching or suboptimal strategy adjustments. Simpler prompt works better.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced scrambler HP threshold from 30 to 25 (-16.7%)
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 88 - Regression. Scrambler HP threshold 25 was too aggressive. Unlike aligner and miner optimizations, scrambler role appears to require the higher threshold of 30. Significant regression on seeds 42 (-18%) and 43 (-22%), with only seed 44 showing improvement. The pattern that worked for aligner and miner doesn't apply to scrambler.

failed_attempts.description: Increased RETREAT_MARGIN from 15 to 20 (+33%) to match alpha.0
failed_attempts.notes: Cycle 85 - Regression. RETREAT_MARGIN=20 too conservative, caused agents to retreat too early. Previously tested in cac8e71, now properly reverted. The wider enemy detection (_NEAR_ENEMY_RADIUS=20) already adds +5 margin when near enemy territory, so base margin of 20 is excessive.
Delta Cycle 80 - Testing after codebase reset. Stall threshold 14 regressed in new context (previous testing showed 14 optimal, but other parameters have changed).

failed_attempts.description: Reduced hub camp distance from 3 to 2 (-33%) to require agents to be closer to hub for camping
failed_attempts.notes: Delta Cycle 83 - Regression. Tighter distance requirement reduced hub camping effectiveness, particularly damaging on seed 46. Context matters: with HP threshold at 85 (not 100), distance 3 works better than 2.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced early retreat window from 150 to 120 steps (-20%)
failed_attempts.notes: Delta Cycle 84 - Regression. Shorter early retreat window reduced performance, particularly on seed 46. The longer 150-step window provides better early game flexibility in current context.

last_improvement.description: Increased JUNCTION_AOE_RANGE from 10 to 20 (+100%) to match alpha.0
last_improvement.notes: Cycle 91 - Alpha.0 parity achievement. Wider enemy AOE radius improves agent survival near enemy junctions. Particularly strong on seed 42 (+16.6%). This parameter controls enemy detection range for retreat decisions, targeting, and pressure calculations. New baseline: 25.42.
